reasoning: Magnus Carlsen has held the FIDE number 1 ranking continuously since 2011. As of the November 2025 Wikipedia snapshot, he leads world number 2 (Hikaru Nakamura) by roughly 26 Elo points and number 3 (Fabiano Caruana) by 44 points. FIDE updates official ratings monthly, so Wikipedia's FIDE rankings page on November 16 will almost certainly reflect the same November list (P=0.98). For December 9, we will see the December list; overturning a 26-point Elo deficit in one month at the super-GM level is extremely rare, usually requiring a dominant tournament performance by the #2 and a collapse by the #1 (P=0.95). By February 2026, slightly more rating fluctuation is possible, but Carlsen remains an overwhelming favorite to remain at #1, having not relinquished the top spot in over a decade (P=0.90). Uncertainties predictably increase slightly over time, hence the tapering probabilities.
